concern.description: Required (non-null) when status is 'blocked': what the contract asks for, what makes it impossible as specified, and what would need to change in the plan. Always null when status is 'implemented'.

assumptions.description: Optional. Smallest-reasonable-assumption notes made to resolve a contract ambiguity (not a codebase-understanding question).

blocked_on.description: Optional, meaningful only when status is 'blocked'. 'contract' (the default if omitted): the contract itself is unsatisfiable as specified — settable by either role. 'tests' (implementor only, TDD): the implementor is confident the authored tests — not the contract — contradict the contract; the driver routes this back to the test author instead of retrying the implementor. 'implementation' (test_implementor only, TDD, and only on a repair pass): the test author verified the authored tests are correct and the production code — not the tests — is what must change; the driver routes this to the implementor instead of re-running the tester. It is inert during the RED phase (a 'blocked' first/red-not-confirmed authoring pass falls through to red_test regardless of blocked_on), so set it only when re-entered on a repair pass. Not schema-enforced against status or role, matching this schema's existing concern/status convention.
